svg.text: Defining player perspective via camera templates
Camera renders the Player's perspective in the game, defining what the Player sees on screen.
In Beyond Mode, camera rules are defined through camera templates. Each template represents a distinct Camera State.
At runtime, each Player always has one active template. Active templates can differ between Players (Co-Op Mode).
Switch active template via Node Graph at runtime; template parameters cannot be modified dynamically.
Access via Manage Main Camera in system menu
Editor interface divided into three sections:
Preview and configure parameters of current Camera Template
Indicates role in gameplay; used as
Character. Editing params, collapsed by default.
Default Effective Target: assigned via Class
Mode determines core camera rules; params vary by mode
Over-the-shoulder view, behind and right of character.
Line of Sight Range / Horizontal Angle / Pitch Angle Range
No: Camera may be pushed by collisions (stutter/shifts).
Yes: Ignores collisions but may move inside models.
Camera at character's eye level, simulating first-person view.
Same editable parameters as other camera modes (FOV, offset, etc.)
Similar to Classic Mode but with editable detailed settings.
Full parameter customization available for the user.
Use Node Graphs to dynamically switch Camera Templates at runtime.
Target Units are a list of Player Entities. Reference template by name.
When disabled, following params cannot be modified.
1. Add [Object Camera] general component to a dynamic object or creation
2. Choose a preconfigured object camera template in the Select Camera field. Use Node Graphs to switch to it at runtime.
VII. Tutorial: Creating Game Cameras in Different Perspectives
Feature: Create different camera angles (e.g. top-down view)
Open in-game editor > ESC menu > Manage Main Camera editing interface
Set [Basic Settings - Mode] to desired camera (e.g. 2.5D Camera for top-down)
Set [Default Target] to the class that needs modification. Alternatively, configure in Class settings.
VIII. Extended: Switching Camera After Interaction
Feature: Camera switching mechanism (first-person / top-down via interaction)
Configure 2 main cameras: [First-Person Camera] and [2.5D Camera] with custom names
Create prefab as interactive object. Add [Component > Tab] with options and trigger area
Node graph: When Tab Selected -> Binary Branch -> Check Option ID -> Switch Camera Template by name
Camera Template Name in node = custom camera name from Step 1
Mount node graph on prefab, save, place in scene, test in Test Play
Tip: Camera can also be configured in Class settings (same result as Main Camera editor).
Template parameters cannot be modified dynamically at runtime; only template switching is supported via Node Graphs.
AI Generated, based on Craftspeople Academy. Version: Luna 7
